exercises.title: ICE PROBE
exercises.brief: Is the key in the sorted vault?
promptLines: `sorted` is ascending. Define **contains(sorted: number[], x: number): boolean** using BINARY search.
starterLines: function contains(sorted: number[], x: number): boolean {
// TODO: lo/hi window, compare the midpoint
solutionLines: function contains(sorted: number[], x: number): boolean {
if (sorted[mid] < x) lo = mid + 1; else hi = mid - 1;

tests.name: present
tests.code: assert(contains([1,3,5,7], 5) === true && contains([1,3,5,7], 1) === true && contains([1,3,5,7], 7) === true);

tests.name: absent
tests.code: assert(contains([1,3,5,7], 4) === false && contains([], 9) === false && contains([2], 1) === false);

exercises.hint: lo<=hi loop; compare midpoint; move lo or hi.
exercises.lore: Halve the space until ICE cracks.

exercises.title: SIGNAL SQUELCH
exercises.brief: Collapse repeated bytes in an ordered stream.
promptLines: `xs` is already sorted, so duplicates sit next to each other. Define a generic **dedupeSorted<T>(xs: T[]): T[]**
returning a new array with each run of equal, *adjacent* elements collapsed to one.
starterLines: // TODO: keep an element only when it differs from the one before it
exercises.hint: Push xs[i] only when i===0 or xs[i] !== xs[i-1].
exercises.lore: Silence the echo; keep one clean pulse per signal.

exercises.title: PHASE SHIFT
exercises.brief: Cycle the buffer leftward, wrapping the overflow.
promptLines: Define a generic **rotateLeft<T>(xs: T[], k: number): T[]** returning a new array shifted left by `k` — the
first `k` elements wrap around to the back. `k` may exceed the length (rotate modulo length); an empty array yields `[]`.
starterLines: function rotateLeft<T>(xs: T[], k: number): T[] {
// TODO: split at k (mod length) and swap the two parts
solutionLines: function rotateLeft<T>(xs: T[], k: number): T[] {
exercises.hint: s = k % length; return xs.slice(s).concat(xs.slice(0, s)). Guard length 0.
exercises.lore: Spin the ring buffer; what falls off the front re-enters the rear.

exercises.title: SLIDING APERTURE
exercises.brief: Scan every contiguous frame of a fixed width.
promptLines: Define a generic **windows<T>(xs: T[], size: number): T[][]** returning every contiguous slice of length `size`,

exercises.title: TALLY SWEEP
exercises.brief: Count how many times the key shows up.
promptLines: `sorted` is ascending. Define **countOccurrences(sorted: number[], x: number): number** returning how many times
